channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Self-regulation of Lewis acid sites on FeOCl toward piezo-self-Fenton reaction for continuous hydroxyl radicals generation
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70327-0

Designing efficient piezocatalysts requires understanding how mechanical stress alters surfaces. This study shows stress-induced electric potentials boost surface acidity, activate oxygen, and enable hydrogen-peroxide-free pollutant degradation.
item.dc:title: Self-regulation of Lewis acid sites on FeOCl toward piezo-self-Fenton reaction for continuous hydroxyl radicals generation
item.dc:creator: Haojie Dong
Yuanyi Zhou
Zhi Li
Wei Liu
Shaobin Li
Mingshan Zhu

item.title: Multi-modal characterization of nitrate reduction nano-catalysts with periodic strain distribution
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70447-7

Imaging strain in large catalytic particles with high precision remains a challenge. Here, the authors apply four-dimensional scanning transmission electron microscopy to map strain in perovskite-type hydroxides, revealing correlations that enable high-performance nitrate reduction to ammonia.
item.dc:title: Multi-modal characterization of nitrate reduction nano-catalysts with periodic strain distribution
item.dc:creator: Yiyuan Tao
Xingyu Zheng
Shi Huang
Ershuai Liu
Yangjian Lin
Qike Jiang
Long Yang
Walter S. Drisdell
Tianding Xu
Yao Yang

item.title: Molecular insights into the regulation of GNPTαβ by LYSET
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 11 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70402-6

The authors demonstrate the multifaceted role of LYSET in stabilizing GNPT at the Golgi, by resolving the topology of LYSET and identifying several critical regions for LYSET function and stability.
item.dc:title: Molecular insights into the regulation of GNPTαβ by LYSET
item.dc:creator: Xi Yang
Balraj Doray
Danielle Henn
Varsha Venkatarangan
Benjamin C. Jennings
Zhongzheng Dong
Jiaxuan Liang
Weichao Zhang
Bokai Zhang
Linchen Yu
Liang Chen
Stuart Kornfeld
Ming Li

item.title: In situ synchrotron X-ray scattering reveals organic-mediated scaling mechanisms on desalination membranes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 10 March 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-026-70508-x

Scaling is a major challenge in engineered systems. This study employs in situ synchrotron X-ray scattering to reveal how organic matter alters nucleation and crystallization pathways, thereby regulating gypsum scaling on desalination membranes.
item.dc:title: In situ synchrotron X-ray scattering reveals organic-mediated scaling mechanisms on desalination membranes
item.dc:creator: Zimou Feng
Shu Xu
Jingjing Cao
Zhen Ren
Yi Yang
Jin Jiang
Xunda Feng
Xinglin Lu
